183.reason: [When a single '&' is encountered, the code logs an error but still returns AND ("&&") token, which is incorrect. It should not return a valid token after detecting an error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, the code logs an error but still returns AND ("&&") token, which is incorrect. It should not return a valid token after detecting an error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', the code logs an error but still returns an AND token ("&&"), which is incorrect. It should not return a valid token after detecting an error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Even when a single '&' is encountered (lexical error), the code still returns an AND token ("&&") instead of skipping or handling it as invalid. This causes incorrect token generation for erroneous input like "if(1 & 2)", violating the requirement to only output error info for erroneous programs.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Even when a single '&' is encountered (lexical error), the code still returns an AND token ("&&"), which is incorrect. It should not return a valid token after logging an error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', the code logs an error but still returns an AND token ("&&"), which is incorrect. It should not return a valid token after detecting an error.]

191.reason: [When a single '|' is encountered, the code logs an error but still returns OR ("||") token, which is incorrect. It should not return a valid token after detecting an error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '|' is encountered, the code logs an error but still returns OR ("||") token, which is incorrect. It should not return a valid token after detecting an error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '|', the code logs an error but still returns an OR token ("||"), which is incorrect. It should not return a valid token after detecting an error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: for a single '|', it logs an error but still returns an OR token ("||"), leading to incorrect token output instead of halting token emission for erroneous lexical input.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, when a single '|' is encountered, it logs an error but still returns an OR token ("||"), which is invalid. The method should return null or avoid adding a token in this case.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, when encountering a single '|', the code logs an error but incorrectly returns an OR token ("||"). This leads to invalid token generation for erroneous input.]
